title: Flaky Energizing Passata Braise
description: A malty, flaky Russian dish made with passata and braise, perfect for a energizing breakfast or lunch. This recipe features a unique combination of oats and eggs.
instructions: Heat a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the oats, caraway seeds, sugar, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Gradually add in the passata and water, stirring continuously. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cook, stirring occasionally, for 20-25 minutes, or until the oats have broken down and the mixture has thickened.
Continue cooking for an additional 5-7 minutes, until the eggs are fully cooked and the mixture is smooth.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Use a spatula to transfer the hot braise to individual serving dishes or a large serving platter.
Garnish with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
Serve the Flaky Energizing Passata Braise hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Cover with a lid or plastic wrap to keep warm while serving.
Reheat in the microwave or over low heat if needed.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
